_comment: Configuration for trend-scout.py — GitHub-native daily trend scouting.

seed_keywords: AI coding session knowledge base sqlite

lanes._comment: Adjacent AI-dev tooling lane — language-agnostic, broader keyword surface. Surfaces repos like jcode (Rust coding-agent harness) that are architecturally adjacent but miss the primary Python/knowledge-base filter. Lower min_stars (2) and 365-day lookback catch emerging projects. Keyword entries may use full GitHub Search qualifiers when a targeted composite query outperforms plain free-text.
lanes.name: adjacent-ai-dev
keywords: AI coding agent harness CLI tool

analysis._comment: GitHub Models LLM analysis path (direction 2). Set enabled=true and export GITHUB_MODELS_TOKEN locally, or set token_env=GITHUB_TOKEN in Actions with permissions.models=read.

veto._comment: Rowboat-style veto gate applied after README enrichment. require_domain_signals>=1 skips repos with no grounded domain signals. min_distinct_learnings>=2 requires at least two distinct insight families so single generic/already-done bullets are skipped.

run_control._comment: Grace window prevents re-running within N hours (recommended: 20 for a daily schedule). Set grace_window_hours=0 to disable. state_file defaults to .trend-scout-state.json next to trend-scout.py. In CI (GitHub Actions), the workflow caches this file between runs via actions/cache so the grace window actually persists across ephemeral runners. Use --force to bypass.
